text: When Abram was ninety-nine, the LORD appeared to him and said, I am God Almighty; walk before me and be blameless.
God said he would make his covenant between them and multiply Abram greatly.
Abram fell face down, and God spoke with him.
God said his covenant was with him: he would be the father of a multitude of nations.
His name would no longer be Abram but Abraham, for he would be father of many nations.
God would make him exceedingly fruitful, and kings would come from him.
God would establish this everlasting covenant with him and his offspring after him, to be their God.
God would give him and his offspring the land of Canaan as an everlasting possession, and would be their God.
God told Abraham he and his offspring must keep this covenant throughout their generations.
Every male among them was to be circumcised — this was the covenant's sign.
They were to circumcise the flesh of their foreskins as a sign of the covenant.
Every male, at eight days old, was to be circumcised throughout the generations, including servants and foreigners.
Whether born in the house or bought, they must be circumcised; the covenant would be in their flesh forever.
Any uncircumcised male would be cut off from his people for breaking the covenant.
God told Abraham that Sarai's name would now be Sarah.
God would bless her and give Abraham a son by her; she would become nations, and kings of peoples would come from her.
Abraham fell on his face and laughed, asking whether a hundred-year-old man could father a child, or ninety-year-old Sarah bear one.
Abraham asked God that Ishmael might simply live before him.
God said Sarah would bear him a son to be named Isaac, and the covenant would be established with him and his offspring.
God said he had heard about Ishmael too, and would bless him greatly, making him father of twelve princes and a great nation.
But God's covenant would be established with Isaac, whom Sarah would bear at this time next year.
When God finished speaking with him, he went up from Abraham.
That same day Abraham took Ishmael and every male in his household and circumcised them, as God had told him.
Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised.
Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised.
Abraham and Ishmael were circumcised that very day.
All the men of his household, born in the house or bought from foreigners, were circumcised with him.
